metadata.title:
SPOKANE union MEN To


OPPOSE CONVICT LABOR

Want State Roads Builded by the Labor Of Taxpayer


and Free Citizens.

Commissioner Bowlby Plans to Use Free Men Only in


Preparing for Men From Prison.

pages.raw_text:
I'VE are going to use convict
labor in the building of the state
roads this summer and we are go
ing to have them at work at the
stone Quarries and rock crushers
near Marshall Junction, but l don't
think organized labor should COM
plain, as the work Is generally of a
nature for which free common la
boring men care little. State
Highway Commissioner Henry l..
Bowlby.


There is something radically
wrong. State Highway Commits
sioner Bowlby's view of the road
building problem is badly in error,
according to the thinking of the
laboring people, and he may have
trouble in keeping within the good
graces of the states organized labor
bodies. should he go ahead with the
plans to build the state roads with
the help of the convicts.


WILL USE FREE MEN FIRST.


1t is the plan of Mr.. Bowlby at
the present time to use free men
only in the construction of the
stone quarry buildings and stockade
on the line Of the Northern Pacific
near Marshall Junction After that
crushed rock will be loaded by the
convicts on the cars for shipment
to different parts Of central Wash
ington. They will be unloaded by
convict labor and the crushed rock
will be put into the roads by con-
vict labor.


Much opposition to this plan is
already voiced among the labor I'M
ionists here. and 1t is likely that the
matter will be carried before the
Spokane Sectional Central Labor
body. where in all probability reso-
lutions condemning the use Of con-
vict labor will be passed and for
warded to those in charge of the
work of improving the roads
throughout the state.


LABoR's HOTTEST FIGHT.


Opposition of convict labor has
been one of the chief issues before
the convention of the state fedora
ton of labor for several sessions
Organized labor's greatest fight In
all parts of the United States has
been waged against the plan and
